data.title: Bit Digital bitcoin production slips 14% in November
data.description:
Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) produced 44.9 bitcoins (BTC-USD) in November, a 14% decline from its production in October, the company said Thursday.

Its active hash rate was ~2.51 EH/s, a 3.3% increase from last month.

The company had 266 servers (2,128 GPUs) actively generating revenue from Bit Digital AI contracts as of Nov. 30, 2024, earning $4.3M of total unaudited GPU Cloud revenue during the month. The amount earned was unchanged from October.

Treasury holdings of bitcoin (BTC-USD) were 813.0 with a fair market value of ~$78.4M, and of ethereum (ETH-USD) was 27,563.3 with a fair market value of $102.1M, as of Nov. 30, 2024. That's up from $54.8M FMV for bitcoin and $69.2M FMV for ether at the end of October.

The company had cash and cash equivalents of ~$332.5M, up from $203.9M at Oct. 31, 2024.

Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) stock increased 1.1% in Thursday after-hours trading, following a 9.2% drop in regular-hours trading.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: DocuSign tops Q3 estimates, guides Q4 topline above consensus
Chevron sees ~$15B organic capex budget for 2025, $2B below this year
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) shares are little changed post-market Thursday after saying it forecasts a FY 2025 organic capital budget of $14.5B-$15.5B and expects to take $1.1B-$1.5B in charges during Q4 for restructuring, asset impairments and other obligations.

Chevron (CVX) said it sees Upstream spending at ~$13B, with about two-thirds allocated to develop its U.S. portfolio, although spending in the Permian Basin is expected to come in lower than the 2024 budget and anticipated at $4.5B-$5B as production growth is reduced in favor of free cash flow.

The company forecasts Downstream capex of ~$1.2B, with two-thirds allocated to the U.S.

Chevron (CVX) said it expects to spend less than $1B on the Tengiz oil field next year as the Kazakhstan expansion project nears completion.

Planned FY 2025 capex represents a $2B Y/Y reduction, and Chairman and CEO Mike Wirth said the "2025 capital budget along with our announced structural cost reductions demonstrate our commitment to cost and capital discipline."

data.title: Beyond Meat shares slipped for the seventh consecutive session
data.description:
Beyond Meat (NASDAQ:BYND) shares dropped for seven straight sessions, as the stock ended 3.7% lower at $4.49 on Thursday.

The plant-based meat company lost 9.9% in the preceding seven sessions. The stock has tumbled nearly 48.7% so far this year, compared to an over 27% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

WPC is down 30% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, BYND has a Hold rating with a score of 3 out of 5. The company received D- in the prospect of profitability, F+ in momentum, while it got a C+ growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, no analysts gave BYND a Buy, while six gave it a Hold rating. Five analysts recommended the stock as Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Earlier in November, the company reported third quarter results above analyst estimates. For 2024, BYND expects net revenues to be in the range of $320M to $330M, vs. consensus of $325.44M.

“Despite Q3 revenue growth and improved gross margins, Beyond Meat's limited liquidity and high debt pose significant risks. The company struggles with taste, health perceptions, and high prices, leading to reduced consumer demand and fewer points of sale,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: Lululemon delivers solid quarterly results, hikes FY24 guidance
data.description:
Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU) delivered solid quarterly results, lifting shares higher in after-hours trading with better-than-expected profit and sales, and double-digit comparable sales growth in its international markets.

The company also raised its FY24 guidance above Wall Street’s expectations and raised its share buyback program by another billion dollars.

Lululemon (LULU) shares are up 9% and climbing.

The athleisure retailer earned a profit of $2.87, up from $1.96 a year ago and 15 cents above expectations. Revenue increased 9% to $2.4B, $40M above estimates. Gross margin increased 150 basis points to 58.5%, while operating margin increased 520 basis points to 20.5%.

Comparable sales remained soft in the U.S., falling 2% from the same quarter last year, but were offset by a 25% surge in international sales, resulting in a 4% increase in comparable sales across all segments. Comparable sales for all three segments were better than expected.

Net revenue in its Americas segment reached $1.77B versus $1.74B estimates, while sales in China topped $318M, beating $314.5M expectations.

Looking ahead to the remainder of the year, Lululemon (LULU) is counting on a strong holiday season to underpin sales for both Q4 and 2024.

“With the majority of the fourth quarter still in front of us, we are focused on deepening engagement with our guests and bringing new consumers into the brand,” said CFO Meghan Frank. As the company has struggled with stale product offerings in previous quarters, it now aims to double net revenue to $12.5B by 2026 with the help of product innovation, guest experience, and market expansion.

For Q4, Lululemon is targeting revenue of $3.475B to $3.510B, representing growth of 8% to 10%, with earnings expected to be between $5.56 to $5.64 per share, both of which bracket the consensus estimates of $3.50B and $5.62 per share.

For 2024, the company raised its guidance with revenue now expected to be in the range of $10.452B and $10.487B from $10.38B to $10.48B, and earnings of $14.08 to $14.16 from initial guidance of $13.95 to $14.15. This exceeds estimates of $10.43B and $14.04, respectively.

data.title: Fannie Mae shifts to annual election of directors
data.description:
Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) recently implemented an annual election schedule for its board, and its conservator, the Federal Housing Finance Agency, executed written stockholder consent, electing all the company's current board members, it said Thursday.

Since 2021, board members appointed for the first time to the board served three-year terms.

Directors elected to the board are: Priscilla Almodovar, Amy E. Alving, Christopher J. Brummer, Renée Lewis Glover, Michael J. Heid, Simon Johnson, Karin J. Kimbrough, Diane N. Lye, Diane C. Nordin, Chetlur S. Ragavan, Manuel Sánchez Rodríguez, Michael A. Seelig, and Michael A. Seelig.

Each director will serve for a term that ends on the date of our next annual meeting of shareholders, or when the conservator next elects our directors by written consent, Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) said.

data.title: HP Enterprise ticks up as AI continues to boost Q4 results, guidance
data.description:
HP Enterprise (HPE) shares rose 3.5% in late trading on Thursday, as the IT giant reported fiscal fourth-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street's expectations, aided in part by the artificial intelligence spending boom.

For the period ending October 31, HP Enterprise said it earned an adjusted $0.58 per share as revenue rose 15.6% year-over-year to come in at $8.5B.

Revenue from its all important server segment, which has seen a boost thanks to AI spending, rose 32% year-over-year to $4.7B. Operating margin for the segment came in at 11.6%, up from 10.1% in the year ago period. Intelligent Edge revenue tumbled 20% year-over-year to $1.1B, while hybrid cloud revenue rose 18% to $1.6B. Financial Services revenue was $893M, up 2% from the prior-year period.

A consensus of analysts expect the company to earn $0.56 per share on $8.26B in revenue.

Looking to the first-quarter of fiscal 2025, HP Enterprise expects to earn between $0.47 and $0.52 per share on an adjusted basis, with revenue rising on a "mid-teens" percentage basis year-over-year. Analysts were expecting adjusted earnings of $0.48 per share.

HP Enterprise also gave an updated on its pending acquisition of Juniper Networks (JNPR) and said it had received approvals from several "key" jurisdictions, including the European Union, the U.K., India and others. Both companies are working with the U.S. Department of Justice as the agency continues its review of the deal, which is expected to close in the early part of 2025.

The company will host a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.
